items.title: GitHub Copilot Adds Kimi K2.7 Code as First Open-Weight Model Picker Option

key_claims.claim: First open-weight model in GitHub Copilot model picker
Hosted on Microsoft Azure at provider list pricing (usage-based billing)
Rollout: Pro, Pro+, Max first; Business/Enterprise over coming weeks
Off by default for Business and Enterprise; admin policy enable required

caveats: GitHub advises reviewing open-weight models against security, compliance, and data-governance requirements before enabling
'Provider list pricing' not defined in changelog; check current Moonshot AI Azure pricing before estimating cost

items.title: Anthropic Removes Steganographic Tracking from Claude Code; Alibaba Bans Tool
items.conflict_of_interest: Anthropic engineer statement is from an interested party; Alibaba characterization as 'backdoor' disputed

key_claims.claim: June 30: Reddit reverse-engineer (LegitMichel777) discovers steganographic tracking code in Claude Code
July 1: Anthropic confirms tracking removal via PR merge — 'an experiment we launched in March'
Alibaba ban effective July 10; employees directed to use Qoder
key_claims.source: South China Morning Post via thenextweb.com

key_claims.claim: 25,000 fraudulent accounts, 28.8M Claude interactions: Alibaba-linked distillation campaign Apr–Jun 2026
key_claims.source: Anthropic Senate Banking Committee letter, June 10 2026

key_claims.claim: Anthropic the only frontier AI lab explicitly restricting PRC-controlled entity access

caveats: FT primary article on loophole closure paywalled; confirmed via thenextweb.com, cryptobriefing, investing.com
Alibaba's characterization as 'backdoor' disputed by Anthropic
Distillation attack attribution based on Anthropic's own Senate letter, not independent verification

items.title: OpenAI Proposes 5% US Government Equity Stake in Alaska-Style Public AI Fund

key_claims.claim: 5% stake at OpenAI's $852B valuation ≈ $42.6B
Altman discussed proposal with Trump, Lutnick, Bessent, Sanders
Discussions described as 'conceptual and early-stage'
Implementation would likely require an act of Congress

cross_references.note: Broader OpenAI vs Anthropic revenue context

caveats: FT primary article and Bloomberg article returned HTTP 403; confirmed via multiple independent secondary outlets
Proposal not formally accepted or submitted to Congress as of July 4
Proposal days after US government delayed GPT-5.6 public release — likely related to government leverage dynamic

items.title: White House Finalizing Voluntary AI Release Review Framework with OpenAI, Google, Anthropic
items.tier_nuance: Primary source is FT (July 3, paywalled). T3 reflects aggregator access, not FT original-reporting quality.

key_claims.claim: White House in 'advanced talks' with OpenAI, Google, Anthropic on voluntary AI release standards
Framework addresses review timelines and threshold definitions for 'covered frontier model' designation
Formal announcement expected 'next week' as of July 3

cross_references.note: June 2 Executive Order establishing the voluntary framework basis
NPR reporting on June 2 EO voluntary structure

caveats: Forward-looking: formal announcement had not occurred as of July 4
'Voluntary' framework — companies cannot be legally compelled to participate
FT primary paywalled; AIToolsRecap summary may omit nuance

items.title: UN Independent Panel Releases First Global Scientific Assessment of AI

key_claims.claim: 40-member independent expert panel
US holds 75% of world's top 500 AI supercomputers; China 15%
AI capability doubling every 4–7 months implies evaluation benchmarks can become outdated within one product cycle
No scientific guarantees that AI systems will not violate instructions
Full comprehensive assessment due 2027; Geneva dialogue July 6–7 2026

caveats: Evidence base for some risks relies on modelling exercises, laboratory studies, and theoretical analysis — not all empirical
Comprehensive assessment with full evidence base due 2027

limitations: FT paywall: Three items sourced from FT (OpenAI stake, Anthropic loopholes, White House standards) — all paywalled. Confirmed via multiple independent secondaries.
bloomberg.com: HTTP 403 for OpenAI stake and Crusoe articles
Login-walled coverage: X timelines, LinkedIn, Discord not directly accessed
US holiday effect: July 4 Independence Day — minimal vendor announcements on this date
Model release coverage thin: No new frontier model GA in window
MCP ecosystem: No in-window (July 1–4) T2+ MCP spec or server releases
Agent frameworks: No new major agent framework releases within window
SEA/Indonesia: Cross-language search conducted; no new in-window T2+ launches
White House framework forward-looking: Announcement expected in July; not yet occurred as of July 4
Claude Code tracking severity contested: Alibaba 'backdoor' vs Anthropic 'anti-abuse experiment' — no independent security analysis published
